channel.title: HoopsHype Rumors - Philadelphia 76ers
channel.description: Philadelphia 76ers trade rumors and free agency buzz from HoopsHype

item.title: Windhorst on Joel Embiid: 'He's not going anywhere'
item.description: NBA Courtside: Brian Windhorst on Joel Embiid questioning his future in Philly: “He has 3 years $192M left on his contract which will begin next season. I can promise him he will definitely be back. He is not going anywhere”

item.title: In a perfect scenario, the 76ers' front office begins …
item.description: In a perfect scenario, the 76ers' front office begins to gauge the trade market on Embiid and George while building a roster centered around Maxey and Edgecombe. But unless Philadelphia is willing to include draft capital, moving off the $300 million in owed salary is difficult. Looking at trades for the 76ers' star veterans would counter what president of basketball operations Daryl Morey stressed at the February trade deadline. "We have some as-close-to-untouchable players as you might have in

item.title: The Sixers concluded he probably would never be a …
item.description: He took heavy criticism for the McCain trade. But objectively, the Sixers concluded he probably would never be a starter in Philadelphia because of Edgecombe’s emergence. Garnering a first-round pick for a projected reserve constitutes a fair deal. The fairer critique is that the 76ers didn’t use the deadline to bring in someone who could immediately help, although Morey explored multiple paths to do so, kicking the tires on a handful of wing options and a big man.

item.title: Jobs of Daryl Morey and Nick Nurse to be evaluated
item.description: That leaves the franchise facing uncomfortable questions and potentially major decisions. The jobs of Morey and head coach Nick Nurse are expected to be evaluated entering the offseason, multiple league sources told The Athletic. As of Sunday’s elimination, team sources said ownership has not made final decisions on either and is likely to take a few days to assess before making any major calls.

item.title: Joel Embiid not on the same page with front office, coaching staff?
item.description: As the season progressed, it became clear the star center didn’t see eye-to-eye with the front office and the coaching staff. In the days before the February trade deadline, he implored the front office to do something other than what he termed “ducking the (luxury) tax.” As such, he — along with others in the locker room — were not happy with the decision to trade second-year guard Jared McCain to the Oklahoma City Thunder, particularly without bringing in a player to replace McCain. In intervi

item.title: The players expressed they felt there wasn’t enough …
item.description: On April 1, the same day as Embiid’s social media post, a handful of players met with members of Philadelphia’s coaching staff before shootaround in Washington to express concern over certain players’ participation in team activities and frustration over the direction of the 76ers’ season, multiple league sources told The Athletic. Among other things, the players expressed they felt there wasn’t enough control of the locker room, according to those sources. The meeting was tense enough to briefl

item.title: Joel Embiid: 'I don’t know who’s going to be here. I don’t even know if I’m going to be here'
item.description: Joel Embiid: But I’m excited about next season. I think VJ is going to be better. I know, and I’m going to talk to him. He’s going to be better. He was amazing for his first year. Tyrese is going to be better, and he’s taken a step every single year. PG, that we saw the last couple weeks — he still got it. And then everybody else, I don’t know who’s going to be here. I don’t even know if I’m going to be here, but whatever happens, happens. But for me, I’m excited about really getting back to mys

item.title: Joel Embiid: We came into the season thinking there was not much left. Now I'm not even worrying about it
item.description: Joel Embiid: I came into this year not knowing where I was going to be, how long I was going to play, or if I was even going to play based on how the knee was the last few years. And, you know, I came in just hoping for the best. I feel like we’re in a position where we figured out the knee hasn’t been an issue. If it wasn’t for the oblique or some of the other stuff that I had, it could have been different as far as how many games I would have played. But, yeah, we came into the season thinking

item.title: VJ Edgecombe: 'Coming into next year a team won't ever leave me open'
item.description: ‪Derek Bodner‬: VJ Edgecombe: "Coming into next year a team won't ever leave me open." "In the regular season I was shooting the ball pretty well. Playoffs come it's a different vibe. Intensity, fire, closeouts are a lot quicker...I'm gonna work. That's all I'm gonna do, whatever I gotta do to get better."

item.title: Josh Hart: I used to think Philly was a sports town, I don't know if it is anymore
item.description: [SNY] Josh Hart says he wasn't surprised at how many Knicks fans were in Philly: "I used to think Philly was a sports town, I don't know if it is anymore"

item.title: It's significantly cheaper to go down to Philly and …
item.description: Yaron Weitzman: It's the prices. Or, more specifically, how insanely expensive it is to go to MSG. That's it. It's significantly cheaper to go down to Philly and watch a game than to go the Garden. For Lots of Knicks fans, this is the only way they can even dream of going to a Knicks playoff game.

item.title: Maybe I’ve got to go to church more, so I don’t have …
item.description: Joel Embiid: But I feel like I still played as hard as I could. I fought hard. I tried to give us a better chance to win. It didn’t happen. So, you know, I look at myself, and I’ve got to be better. Maybe I’ve got to go to church more, so I don’t have those types of things right before the playoffs. I don’t know. Maybe I’m cursed. I don’t know. Maybe Philadelphia is cursed. Yes. But that’s a joke. Just got to keep doing the right things over and over, and hope that at some point it changes.

item.title: Tyrese Maxey said he will debrief the season …
item.description: Gina Mizell: Tyrese Maxey said he will debrief the season by...becoming a new dad. You know this if you're a very online Sixers/NBA fan, but it's really the first time he's addressed in this type of setting. "I’m ready to go home and then just see (what) that’s like, that’s just honestly the facts of it."

item.title: Paul George was asked what he's focusing on this …
item.description: Derek Bodner: Paul George was asked what he's focusing on this summer. "Figuring out if I can get that explosiveness back. It limited me a lot this year. My entire career [was] the jump shot kinda being the jab...I just felt like that [explosiveness] wasn't there this year."

item.title: Daryl Morey is on his way to Chicago for the draft …
item.description: Austin Krell: For those wondering, Daryl Morey is on his way to Chicago for the draft combine. He will not speak tonight or tomorrow.

item.title: Tyrese Maxey on Knicks fans taking over the 76ers arena: ‘It absolutely sucks’
item.description: Derek Bodner: Tyrese Maxey on the Knicks fan takeover in Philly: "It absolutely sucks. It just sucks. That's really all I can say about it, man...There's only one way to put a stop to it: we have to go out there and win these games. It felt louder here for them than it did in the Garden."

item.title: Tyrese Maxey, on VJ Edgecombe's growth into being more …
item.description: Derek Bodner: Tyrese Maxey, on VJ Edgecombe's growth into being more of a perimeter initiator: "I think next year I'm gonna do some things off the ball, and I don't have to be in front of the defense all the time. When you're in front of the defense, it gives them an opportunity to trap you."

item.title: VJ Edgecombe says he does not know where he is …
item.description: Adam Aaronson: VJ Edgecombe says he does not know where he is spending his summer. He has been “on the road for the past six years.” He thinks he’ll spend time in The Bahamas, New York and Texas, where he can work out with Tyrese Maxey.

item.title: “Next year, I’m going to come back better, stronger …
item.description: Adam Aaronson: “Next year, I’m going to come back better, stronger faster, more athletic,” VJ Edgecombe said. “Whatever I got to do, I’m going to go.”

item.title: Kelly Oubre on free agency: 'I've averaged 20 points in this league and still find myself barely getting any contracts'
item.description: Derek Bodner: Kelly Oubre Jr on free agency: "I've averaged 20 points in this league and still find myself barely getting any contracts...I hope I did myself a good service by being more efficient, slowing down, and playing better overall basketball." Oubre says he loves it in Philly.

item.title: Asked Kelly Oubre Jr. about what he is prioritizing as …
item.description: Adam Aaronson: Asked Kelly Oubre Jr. about what he is prioritizing as far as situation is concerned. He said he wants to be “somewhere I’m loved,” then spoke at length about how, as a husband and father of two kids, he wants his children to have a place they can call home.

item.title: VJ Edgecombe, on Joel Embiid's praise: "I really love …
item.description: Derek Bodner: VJ Edgecombe, on Joel Embiid's praise: "I really love my teammates and I really love all the good things they say about me, but I gotta put in the work to continue getting better so what they say can come to pass.'"

item.title: Tyrese Maxey says his finger got a bit better after …
item.description: Adam Aaronson: Tyrese Maxey says his finger got a bit better after the Orlando game. At the end of the second quarter of Game 2 in Boston, he got it caught in a jersey and fell on it. “It hurts bad.” It happened earlier vs. Detroit, too. He’ll do hand therapy over the summer.

item.title: Quentin Grimes says he has not yet thought about his …
item.description: Adam Aaronson: Quentin Grimes says he has not yet thought about his unrestricted free agency this summer. Called his season “solid.”

item.title: Joel Embiid, asked about whether he thinks about his …
item.description: Derek Bodner: Joel Embiid, asked about whether he thinks about his legacy: "I want to win more than anyone. It sucks to lose. I haven't won anything, so that hurts. But to go home and raise [his son], raise my daughter, look at my wife in the eyes and understand I'm a good man...Really, that's all that matters."

item.title: Joel Embiid, on the Knicks: "At times, it's okay to …
item.description: Derek Bodner: Joel Embiid, on the Knicks: "At times, it's okay to just say the other team was better....Gotta get better, from top to bottom. Ownership, front office, players, coaches. Everybody just gotta get better."

item.title: Joel Embiid says he wants to spend his whole career in the 76ers: 'I love Philly'
item.description: Adam Aaronson: Asked about his relationship with the Sixers’ front office, Joel Embiid said “I love Philly,” reiterated his desire to win in Philadelphia and spend his whole career here, and talked about the disappointment of not winning a title. Did not address where he is at with management.

item.title: Joel Embiid with high praise for rookie VJ Edgecombe: …
item.description: Kyle Neubeck: Joel Embiid with high praise for rookie VJ Edgecombe: "That guy is something different, and this was only year one…he has a chance to be extremely special" and hopes to help him continue developing in the months and years ahead

item.title: Nick Nurse, on VJ Edgecombe's rookie season: "That was …
item.description: Derek Bodner: Nick Nurse, on VJ Edgecombe's rookie season: "That was a hell of an impressive rookie season. He's a hell of a player. It's not about shooting, scoring, jumping, all that stuff. He really knows how to play. He really has a leadership quality to him. I think he's a bigtime winner."

item.title: Nick Nurse on Joel Embiid: 'He worked his a** off to get out there and play'
item.description: Derek Bodner: Nick Nurse, on Joel Embiid: "He worked his ass off to get out there and play. I think it was really difficult for him, especially [in Game 3]. He gave us everything he could. I commend him."

item.title: Nick Nurse, on New York's open 3s: "I think we came …
item.description: Derek Bodner: Nick Nurse, on New York's open 3s: "I think we came out ready to go, we executed well on the offensive end and shots didn't do in, and it bothered our guys. We just couldn't get over that. I think that dispirited them at the start."

item.title: Knicks sweep Philadelphia in lights-out shooting game
item.description: Tim Bontemps: Final: Knicks 144, Sixers 114 An absolute demolition by the Knicks, who sweep Philly, pick up a seventh consecutive victory overall & advance to the Eastern Conference finals for a second straight year. Philly has not advanced to the conference finals for a quarter century.

item.title: Best Knicks playoff series ever in point differential: …
item.description: Jorge Sierra: Best Knicks playoff series ever in point differential: 2026 vs. Atlanta: +105. 2026 vs. Philadelphia: +89. Yes, their two best marks in NBA history have happened in this postseason

item.title: Knicks will sweep a best-of-7 series for the first …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Knicks will sweep a best-of-7 series for the first time since 1999 & the third time in team history. Last time the Knicks swept a series, they went to the Finals...

item.title: Knicks fans heckiling Joel Embiid at the free throw …
item.description: CJ Fogler: Knicks fans heckiling Joel Embiid at the free throw line in Philly

item.title: Knicks tie playoff record for most three-pointers in a quarter
item.description: ESPN Insights: The Knicks are rolling early. They have tied the NBA playoff record for most 3-pt FG in any quarter (11).

item.title: This crowd is overwhelmingly -- I mean overwhemingly …
item.description: Stefan Bondy: This crowd is overwhelmingly -- I mean overwhemingly -- Knicks fans. It's really like a game at MSG.

item.title: OG Anunoby out vs. Sixers
item.description: The Knicks are playing it cautiously. OG Anunoby (right hamstring strain) is out for their Game 4 clash against the 76ers Sunday afternoon at Xfinity Mobile Arena as they look to finish off a series sweep. He also missed the Knicks’ Game 3 win on Friday with the injury. He is still considered day-to-day and the Knicks have been optimistic that he won’t miss much time. Imaging revealed that it is a “very minor” strain, The Post’s Stefan Bondy previously reported.

item.title: Brown shares that OG Anunoby is out for today's Game 4 …
item.description: Knicks Videos: Mike Brown shares that OG Anunoby is out for today's Game 4 matchup with the 76ers:

item.title: From the latest Physicality / Playing Hard report: …
item.description: Roland Beech: From the latest Physicality / Playing Hard report: Knicks-Sixers... Jalen dominated the rookie

item.title: The San Antonio Spurs were a ratings drag during the …
item.description: The San Antonio Spurs were a ratings drag during the days of Tim Duncan, but the Victor Wembanyama era promises to be the opposite. Sunday’s Spurs-Timberwolves second round NBA playoff Game 4 averaged a combined 7.9 million viewers across Nielsen and Adobe Analytics, trailing only Sixers-Celtics Game 7 on NBC following the Kentucky Derby (10.99M) as the most-watched game of the ongoing NBA Playoffs.

item.title: 76ers owner Josh Harris on Daryl Morey dismissal: We have failed to deliver in the way this city deserves
item.description: Sixers Managing Partner Josh Harris: “After speaking with Daryl, we determined that it was time for a fresh start. Bob Myers will lead the process of identifying a new leader and I believe his experience in constructing four NBA championship teams will be a valuable resource to our organization. To our fans, your frustration and disappointment are understandable and warranted. We have fallen well short of our own expectations and failed to deliver in the way this city deserves. That bothers me d

item.title: Bob Myers added: “I know how much the 76ers mean to …
item.description: Bob Myers added: “I know how much the 76ers mean to the City of Philadelphia, and it’s important we find the right leader to shape the future of this team,” Myers said. “The process will start immediately, and we will be thorough and deliberate in our evaluations. I believe this is a destination for top talent across the league and look forward to solidifying our infrastructure moving forward.”

item.title: Nick Nurse will remain as 76ers head coach
item.description: Shams Charania: Head coach Nick Nurse will continue in his role into a fourth season with the 76ers, sources tell ESPN. Four-time NBA championship architect Bob Myers, the president of sports for Harris Blitzer Sports & Entertainment, will be tasked to lead the search for the new head executive for the 76ers and he will oversee the franchise's basketball department in the interim.

item.title: Daryl Morey out as 76ers president of basketball operations
item.description: Shams Charania: BREAKING: Daryl Morey is out as president of basketball operations with the Philadelphia 76ers after six seasons, sources tell ESPN. Morey and 76ers owners Josh Harris and David Blitzer met on Tuesday evening in Philadelphia and the decision was made to part ways.

item.title: Sources say Morey had two expensive years left on his …
item.description: League sources say Morey had two expensive years left on his contract through the 2027-28 season thanks to an extension he signed in December 2023. Sixers ownership nonetheless decided it would stomach the cost of parting ways immediately.

item.title: One source with knowledge of the situation insisted …
item.description: One source with knowledge of the situation insisted Tuesday that the widely criticized trade of McCain to Oklahoma City for a 2026 first-round pick via Houston and three second-round picks was not the proverbial last straw. Ownership very much approved the McCain deal and sources say that Sixers leadership feels quite confident in its post-Morey scouting ability to ensure that a quality player is selected at No. 22 overall next month.

item.title: Andre Iguodala not a candidate for Sixers' job
item.description: I'm also told Myers will interview candidates internally as well as externally to take over … but also that it's far too early to name any possible favorites for the role. One item, however, that has been spelled out rather definitively: For all the recent rumblings about Myers' affinity for former Golden State Finals MVP Andre Iguodala, I’m told Philadelphia does not plan to consider the ex-Sixer and current executive director of the NBA Players Association as a potential candidate for its new

item.title: Sunday’s Spurs-Timberwolves second round NBA playoff …
item.description: The San Antonio Spurs were a ratings drag during the days of Tim Duncan, but the Victor Wembanyama era promises to be the opposite. Sunday’s Spurs-Timberwolves second round NBA playoff Game 4 averaged a combined 7.9 million viewers across Nielsen and Adobe Analytics, trailing only Sixers-Celtics Game 7 on NBC following the Kentucky Derby (10.99M) as the most-watched game of the ongoing NBA Playoffs. The last non-clinching early round game to average as large an audience was Warriors-Lakers Game

item.title: The Sixers are going to be higher hiring a new head of …
item.description: Shams Charania: The Sixers are going to be higher hiring a new head of basketball operations, someone that will now carry it forwards from a leadership perspective, but that person will partner with Bob Myers, who's the four-time championship executive and architect of the Golden State Warriors, and now he's the president of sports for multiple teams on the portfolios of owners Josh Harris and David Blitzer. Remember, Bob Myers oversaw the hiring of Adam Peters with the Washington commanders. He

item.title: 76ers tried to make trades for Donte DiVincenzo, Naji Marshall, Ryan Dunn, Brandin Podziemski at deadline
item.description: Among other players the source said the Sixers made runs at, unsuccessfully: Naji Marshall of the Dallas Mavericks, Donte DiVincenzo of the Minnesota Timberwolves, Brandin Podziemski of the Golden State Warriors and Ryan Dunn of the Phoenix Suns. None of those players were moved.

item.title: The Sixers, the source said, offered the Chicago Bulls …
item.description: The Sixers, the source said, offered the Chicago Bulls multiple second-round picks for reserve big man Jalen Smith; Chicago held onto him. Their primary target, PhillyVoice learned, was Aaron Wiggins, one of Oklahoma City's many trusted reserves. There was hope that the talks with the Thunder about McCain could lead to a deal also involving Wiggins – the Sixers would have needed to send out multiple other players in the process – but ultimately the deal ended up centering around McCain, with Wig

item.title: Krejčí would certainly not have been a "needle-mover" …
item.description: Krejčí would certainly not have been a "needle-mover" – he was not a playoff rotation player for Portland – but he would have represented a new lever Nurse could pull. He would have given the Sixers a bit more optionality on the wing and some much-needed three-point shooting. But the Sixers misread Krejčí's market and, in turn, mistimed their efforts to acquire him, the source said. When the deadline came and went without the Sixers adding a single player to their roster, that relatively min

item.title: Daryl's arrogance has backfired on him repeatedly over …
item.description: Tim MacMahon: Daryl's arrogance has backfired on him repeatedly over the years. He rubs people the wrong way and I would say his biggest failing as a basketball decision maker is he's never been a guy who valued, or even frankly considered, the human element. He does not believe in basketball chemistry.

item.title: "Luka Doncic is starting to get in that territory of …
item.description: First Take: "Luka Doncic is starting to get in that territory of Joel Embiid. When you need him the most, and it comes postseason time, he's not available." 👀 — @KendrickPerkins on if the Lakers should prioritize Luka or LeBron

item.title: Kingston Flemings compares his game to Tyrese Maxey and De'Aaron Fox
item.description: So, what was the advice he gave you going into this experience? Kingston Flemings: Yeah, definitely just putting my head down and working and just being me at the end of the day. You can't be someone else. He had a talk with me during the year that I was trying to be to Allen Iverson. Don't be Allen Iverson, be Kingston Flemings. So really just being myself and if myself is going to be good enough for a team and when that team drafts me, I'm going to show them who I am. So that's really what coa

item.title: "We're looking forward to welcoming [Joel Embiid] back …
item.description: Austin Krell: Josh Harris: "We're looking forward to welcoming [Joel Embiid] back on our team."

item.title: "I'm a big believer in character and leadership. I'm …
item.description: Austin Krell: "I'm a big believer in character and leadership. I'm looking for a person that embodies those things." - Bob Myers on what he's looking for in the next President of BB Ops

item.title: Josh Harris says there is no issue with the luxury …
item.description: Ky Carlin: Josh Harris says there is no issue with the luxury tax. He added that the front office always has the green light to go into the tax. #Sixers

item.title: Bob Myers says at a Sixers press conference in the …
item.description: Marc Stein: Bob Myers says at a Sixers press conference in the wake of Daryl Morey's dismissal that he will communicate "five to seven days a week" with the new head of basketball operations hired to succeed Morey. The goal, Myers says, is to have a hire in place for the June 23-24 draft.

item.title: “Look, I think Nick had the team played hard, and …
item.description: Ky Carlin: Josh Harris on the job Nick Nurse has done so far: “Look, I think Nick had the team played hard, and there were a lot of things that happened. I think we all saw where there were various rosters that were available and rosters that weren't available.” #Sixers

item.title: “Tuesday was a difficult day for me. I consider Daryl …
item.description: Ky Carlin: Josh Harris: “Tuesday was a difficult day for me. I consider Daryl Morey a friend, and he did a lot of positive things for our franchise. Made the playoffs five or six years, including the conference semifinals.” #Sixers

item.title: "We should be graded on the ultimate result of …
item.description: Adam Aaronson: Myers on the Jared McCain trade: "We should be graded on the ultimate result of transactions."

item.title: Asked about his and Myers' involvement in the McCain …
item.description: Adam Aaronson: Asked about his and Myers' involvement in the McCain trade, Harris says the front office made the recommendation to ownership – including Myers. "We were involved in it. It was part of a bigger plan... We don't know the outcome of that trade right now."

item.title: "I think putting all of the focus on one player is the …
item.description: Adam Aaronson: Josh Harris on Joel Embiid: "I think putting all of the focus on one player is the wrong place to be." Harris calls Embiid a "warrior" and says "we're looking forward to welcoming him back on our team."

item.title: Myers says the Sixers' draft history is strong and he …
item.description: Adam Aaronson: Myers says the Sixers' draft history is strong and he does not want to mess it up. "Maxey is the ultimate success story in that range," he says of the Sixers' upcoming No. 22 overall pick.

item.title: Harris, asked about fans being thrown out of games for …
item.description: Adam Aaronson: Harris, asked about fans being thrown out of games for yelling "SELL THE TEAM" and other frustrations in the city, says "nobody is more frustrated than I am." Says his goal remains "pushing through" the second round and into the NBA Finals. "I'm sweating it really hard."

item.title: "Depth may be more important than it's ever been... We …
item.description: Adam Aaronson: Myers, asked if it remains tenable to still have three max players and build enough depth to win a title, says this year's Sixers clearly could not do it. "Depth may be more important than it's ever been... We have to look at what happened this year and be honest about."

item.title: Bob Myers says he would not work for someone who will …
item.description: Austin Krell: Bob Myers says he would not work for someone who will not allow the moves necessary to improve the team because of luxury tax concerns.

item.title: "I want you to know, no one is more frustrated than me …
item.description: Erin Grugan: Josh Harris to the Sixers fans: "I want you to know, no one is more frustrated than me that we haven't achieved our goals ... We owe it to you and the city to be better."

item.title: Jamahl Mosley, who coached the Orlando Magic for five …
item.description: Jamahl Mosley, who coached the Orlando Magic for five seasons before he was let go earlier this month, was captured using smelling salts before Orlando’s first Play-In Tournament game against the Philadelphia 76ers last month. “It heightens your awareness,” Mosley said in April. He added it was only his second time using them.

item.title: Sixers owner on Knicks fans taking over Philly: We did everything we could, including buying tickets to keep them out
item.description: This was what happened in the 76ers-Knicks series, as New York took over Xfinity Mobile Arena in Game 4 with chants of “Knicks in four”. This was jarring to hear, as it did not seem like Philly was playing at home at all. 76ers owner Josh Harris made his feelings regarding the matter perfectly clear in a press conference on Thursday night. “Obviously I didn’t like it. It’s our homecourt. We did everything we could, including buying tickets to keep the [Knicks] fans out. It’s on us. The fans supp

item.title: Sixers owner Josh Harris "Obviously I didn't like …
item.description: New York Basketball: Q "How did it feel sitting…with all the Knicks fan that took over?" Sixers owner Josh Harris "Obviously I didn't like it…We did everything we could…to keep the fans out…Let's face it–we got swept–so…there were some Knicks fans…Our job's to come back & win that series next year"

item.title: He estimates the Knicks earned $8 million per home …
item.description: The Knicks generated $115 million from the playoffs last year, according to Seaport Research Partners analyst David Joyce, who covers the publicly traded MSGS. He estimates the Knicks earned $8 million per home game during the first round of the 2026 playoffs from tickets, suites, concessions and merchandise. It jumped to $12 million per game in the second round when the Knicks swept the Philadelphia 76ers.

item.title: "Run it back, load manage Joel [Embiid] as much as …
item.description: Tim Bontemps: As the Philadelphia 76ers embark on their search for a new team president after firing Daryl Morey on Tuesday, the consensus from sources around the league is that no matter whom Bob Myers chooses to run basketball operations, there aren't many levers to pull to significantly improve the team in the short term. "Run it back, load manage Joel [Embiid] as much as they can, be a playoff team and try to stay healthy," a West executive said. "I think there's stuff you can do on the marg

item.title: Since the Game 4 adjustments against Atlanta, he’s …
item.description: Since the Game 4 adjustments against Atlanta, he’s averaging 99 touches per 100 possessions, according to Second Spectrum. Against the Sixers, that number climbed to 103. During the regular season, he averaged only 83. This is a different Karl-Anthony Towns, more in line with the one head coach Mike Brown tried to create early in the season but who could not get comfortable as the hopeful hub of the offense. Now, he’s adopted this style and been one of the best players in the NBA playoffs.

item.title: Draymond Green: 'Bob Myers has been to Daryl Morey almost what Michael Jordan was to Stockton and Malone'
item.description: Draymond Green: “Bob Myers has been to Daryl Morey almost what Michael Jordan was to Stockton and Malone. You’ve done great work. I mean, I’m not saying Daryl Morey is Stockton or Malone, but you’ll get the reference in a second. You’ve done some great work, right? You traded for James Harden when nobody believed, gave him a max contract, and then James Harden proceeded to become who he became. You built a team with the Houston Rockets that was a very good roster, built to beat the Golden State

item.title: The NBA Draft Lottery set a record last weekend, with …
item.description: The NBA Draft Lottery set a record last weekend, with ABC drawing 2.3 million viewers. That led into the Knicks finishing a sweep of the 76ers in the Eastern Conference semifinals. ABC was up 23% from the lottery last year. USA Network averaged 529,000 viewers for its debut WNBA game on Sunday afternoon, featuring Aces-Sparks. The game on Mother’s Day was up 18% from the WNBA’s average on cable TV last season. Aces-Sparks also had the youngest median age of all national WNBA game broadcasts on o

item.title: "It's time for them to part ways, a mutual thing. I …
item.description: Kendrick Perkins: "It's time for them to part ways, a mutual thing. I think it's best for Joel Embiid to go see what another organization has to offer. I think it's best for Joel Embiid to go witness and see what another medical staff is like. Maybe under somebody else, maybe their medical staff might be better than the 76ers' medical staff. We've seen a lot of stories where guys left certain organizations, and all of a sudden you're like, who the f*** is that?"

item.title: Baron Davis: Jalen Brunson is Allen Iverson in this era
item.description: Baron Davis: Jalen Brunson to me is like Allan Iverson in this era. Like nobody can guard him. No matter what you do, he going to get his shot off. He gets hot. You know, it's on Fuego and he can play to the moment.

item.title: There is a widespread belief, given those parameters, …
item.description: There is a widespread belief, given those parameters, that Myers would want to explore trying to hire Onsi Saleh away from Atlanta after Saleh worked under Myers in Golden State before moving on to the Hawks. The likelihood relayed to me, though, is that Saleh is expected to remain with the Hawks.

item.title: Nelson is held in very high esteem in Philadelphia and …
item.description: There has likewise been chatter all season about a likely expanded role for Sixers assistant general manager Jameer Nelson no matter who comes in as Morey's replacement. Nelson is held in very high esteem in Philadelphia and is routinely described as a rising candidate to run his own team someday.

item.title: Jaylen Brown takes a shot at Stephen A. Smith
item.description: On Sunday night, Jaylen Brown took to his FCHWPO Twitch channel to address Smith directly over comments the $40 million talking head made about Brown’s post-Game 7 livestream. And it only took three words to get a feel for where Brown’s response was heading. “F*ck Stephen A,” Brown said during his Twitch stream Sunday night. Smith spoke critically of Brown on ESPN’s “First Take” after Boston surrendered a 3-1 first-round series lead to the Philadelphia 76ers, primarily focusing on the 2024 NBA c

item.title: There’s a fine line between fans being deemed …
item.description: There’s a fine line between fans being deemed passionate vs. obnoxious, and the Celtics’ loyalists are apparently walking it quite well. The former race wasn’t nearly as close as the latter, though, with the TD Garden crowd winning this one going away. As for the Philly fans being pegged as the over-the-top type, it’s hard to blame them for taking out all these decades of frustration on visiting teams. The Sixers haven’t won a title since 1983, and haven’t been to a Finals since the Allen Iverso

item.title: Dubai BC interested in Guerschon Yabusele
item.description: Dubai BC is reportedly very interested in bringing Guerschon Yabusele back to Europe after his last two seasons in the NBA. The French forward played for the Philadelphia 76ers, New York Knicks and Chicago Bulls, confirming himself as an experienced and physical profile for the European market. Dubai’s interest would fit the club’s ambitious EuroLeague project.

item.title: Mike Gansey, Nick U'Ren among candidates for Philadelphia 76ers front-office position
item.description: Marc Stein: Cavaliers GM Mike Gansey and Phoenix Mercury GM Nick U'ren, who worked for the Warriors before his move to the WNBA, have emerged as names to watch in Philadelphia's front office search, @TheSteinLine has learned. Adding to our existing Philly coverage: tinyurl.com/2hw5e8pj

item.title: Franz Wagner, Bogdan Bogdanović and VJ Edgecombe are …
item.description: Cody Taylor: Franz Wagner, Bogdan Bogdanović and VJ Edgecombe are among the players who will be special guest mentors at adidas Eurocamp 2026 on June 5-7 in Treviso, Italy. The following teams and athletes will participate:

item.title: “Josh desperately wants Bob to run the team,” one …
item.description: “Josh desperately wants Bob to run the team,” one source said. “Bob desperately does not want to run the team.” A Sixers source refutes the characterization of Harris’ desires as “desperate.”

item.title: League sources confirmed that Myers has no plans to …
item.description: In addition to dealing with the Sixers, Myers will be expected to continue to oversee the progress of the other franchises. League sources confirmed that Myers has no plans to move his family. He has three young daughters.

item.title: IT’S BEEN A LONG TIME COMING… PROUD TO BE A GOONER …
item.description: Joel Embiid: IT’S BEEN A LONG TIME COMING… PROUD TO BE A GOONER #COYG

item.title: Cooper Flagg, Dylan Harper, Kon Knueppel headline All-Rookie First Team
item.description: NBA Communications: The 2025-26 Kia NBA All-Rookie First Team: Cedric Coward, @memgrizz VJ Edgecombe, @sixers Cooper Flagg, @dallasmavs Dylan Harper, @spurs Kon Knueppel, @hornets

item.title: Jameer Nelson to be promoted in Philly?
item.description: From his beginnings in Chester, Pa., to his storied career at Saint Joseph’s and a terrific NBA lifespan for a small guard, to a front-office career with the Philadelphia 76ers, Jameer Nelson has permanently etched his name in Philly hoops lore. And it looks like he’s going to take another step forward. Multiple league sources tell The Athletic that Nelson, among others, is a candidate to become the 76ers’ next president of basketball operations. He is Philadelphia’s strongest internal candidate

item.title: One of the most notable, current Cleveland Cavaliers …
item.description: As Bob Myers, president of sports for Harris Blitzer Sports & Entertainment, leads the search, several candidates have emerged. One of the most notable, current Cleveland Cavaliers general manager Mike Gansey, has been an executive in Cleveland for almost a decade and is considered a strong candidate to take over for Morey, according to league sources. He was once a standout guard for a West Virginia team that surprisingly went all the way to the Elite Eight of the NCAA Tournament in 2006.

item.title: Nick U’Ren, general manager for the WNBA’s Phoenix …
item.description: Nick U’Ren, general manager for the WNBA’s Phoenix Mercury, is expected to interview for the position, according to league sources. Current Minnesota Timberwolves general manager Matt Lloyd is also expected to interview. Lloyd, league sources say, has the backing of a few key members of the Sixers’ organization. He was a finalist for the Chicago Bulls front-office opening that eventually went to Bryson Graham. Trent Redden, currently the general manager with the LA Clippers, has also emerged as

item.title: Hawks denied Sixers permission to interview Onsi Saleh
item.description: Myers attempted to interview Atlanta Hawks general manager Onsi Saleh, according to league sources. But the Hawks, last Thursday, denied the Sixers permission for the interview.

item.title: Jared McCain on getting traded by 76ers: I started crying when I found out, I still miss Philadelphia to this day
item.description: On Feb. 3, however, he found out he was being traded to the Thunder. “I was on the bus with the Sixers and we were heading to the airport in San Francisco. We just played Golden State,” McCain said. “I texted my agent for a gym in L.A. and he called me, and I think he’s calling me about the gym. He is like, ‘They’re about to trade you.’ Then [then-Sixers president Daryl Morey] called me five minutes later and told me I was traded. It was pretty wild, and I started crying immediately. “Those are

item.title: Ben Simmons wins championship in sportfishing
item.description: Ben Simmons is now a champion ... in sportfishing. On Sunday, the South Florida Sails captured the Sport Fishing Championship Blue Marlin Open at Walker's Cay in the Bahamas. Helping South Florida to the title was none other than Simmons, a co-owner and angler for the club. Better known for his NBA career, Simmons was taken by the Philadelphia 76ers with the first pick in the 2016 draft and went on to play seven seasons in the league.

item.title: Jared McCain on Daryl Morey trading him: 'It's never to prove anybody wrong'
item.description: Michael Scotto: Jared McCain on Daryl Morey trading him: “It’s never to prove anybody wrong. I try to keep a positive outlook. I like proving my support system right. Daryl’s still the guy that drafted me, so I’ll always have love for him for that. He believed in me enough to take me at No. 16.”

item.title: Eddie Johnson: The King of Analytics, couldn’t use …
item.description: StatMuse: Fun Fact: Jared McCain has scored more points in the Conference Finals than the last six 76ers lottery picks combined. Eddie Johnson: The King of Analytics, couldn’t use this guy in Philly.

item.title: Lakers and Mavericks interested in 76ers’ Prosper Karangwa
item.description: While Nelson appears to be the safest of any current 76ers executive on staff, it’s unclear what’s next for others on the staff. For example, Prosper Karangwa has drawn interest from the Lakers and Mavericks, league sources told HoopsHype.

item.title: The Los Angeles Lakers have expressed interest in …
item.description: The Los Angeles Lakers have expressed interest in several candidates for their front office openings, including Timberwolves assistant general manager Steve Senior, 76ers assistant general manager Prosper Karangwa, Heat vice president of player personnel Eric Amsler, Jazz vice president of player personnel Bart Taylor, and others, league sources told HoopsHype.

item.title: Mavericks interested in adding Patrick Englebrecht, Prosper Karangwa to their front office
item.description: According to league sources, the Mavericks have expressed interest in adding Philadelphia’s Prosper Karangwa to their front office, and Toronto’s director of global scouting, Patrick Englebrecht, is also a candidate to reunite with Ujiri in Dallas after they worked together since the 2013-14 season.

item.title: 76ers assistant general manager Jameer Nelson has also …
item.description: Thus far, external candidates, including Cleveland’s Mike Gansey, Minnesota’s Matt Lloyd, and Phoenix Mercury general manager Nick U'Ren, have emerged. Internally, 76ers assistant general manager Jameer Nelson has also positioned himself well to remain with the organization and be in the mix for a possible promotion, league sources told HoopsHype.

item.title: Bob Myers is expected to be involved in all major …
item.description: Ultimately, whoever is hired will have to work with head coach Nick Nurse, who was retained. Nurse has two years left on his contract, league sources told HoopsHype. In addition, Bob Myers is expected to be involved in all major transactional decisions for the 76ers moving forward, even after a new basketball operations leader is hired.

item.title: Chet Holmgren, Jalen Johnson, Jamal Murray selected to All-NBA Third Team
item.description: NBA Communications: The 2025-26 Kia All-NBA Third Team: Jalen Duren, @DetroitPistons Chet Holmgren, @okcthunder Jalen Johnson, @ATLHawks Tyrese Maxey, @sixers Jamal Murray, @nuggets

item.title: Super max eligible next offseason but only if he earns …
item.description: Bobby Marks: All-NBA implications: - Tyrese Maxey: Super max eligible next offseason but only if he earns All-NBA in 2026-27 - Jalen Duren: Eligible to negotiate up to 30% of the cap.

item.title: Sources say the final step in this process will be …
item.description: Jake Fischer: Bob Myers conducted meetings for the Sixers’ first phase of their lead executive search while out in Los Angeles. Sources say the final step in this process will be select candidates interviewing with Joshua Harris’ ownership group in person.

item.title: Another name that I’ve heard connected with the Lakers …
item.description: Dan Woike: Another name that I’ve heard connected with the Lakers over the last few days is Philadelphia 76ers player personal exec Prosper Karangwa.

item.title: How quickly it took the Knicks to build a 20-point …
item.description: Zach Kram: How quickly it took the Knicks to build a 20-point lead in potential closeout games this spring: vs. Atlanta: 11 minutes vs. Philly: 11 minutes vs. Cleveland: 13.5 minutes

item.title: Knicks are the first team in NBA history to have three …
item.description: ‪Keerthika Uthayakumar‬: Knicks are the first team in NBA history to have three 20+ point series clinching wins in a single playoff run. 51-point win in Game 6 vs the Hawks 30-point win in Game 4 vs the 76ers 37-point win in Game 4 vs the Cavs

item.title: Their three largest playoff series margins of victory …
item.description: ‪Law Murray 📘‬: The New York Knicks have been a National Basketball Association team from the beginning of the damn league Their three largest playoff series margins of victory have been this year +105 vs Hawks +89 vs 76ers +77 vs Cavaliers previous high was +54 in 1972 East semis vs Bullets.

item.title: On average, top-five picks from 2000 through 2019 …
item.description: The dream of a decade-plus of production for a franchise cornerstone also doesn't materialize very often. On average, top-five picks from 2000 through 2019 lasted 5.1 seasons with their original team, with 56% lasting until their fifth year (and their second contract) and 44% leaving before then. Only 6% played at least 10 seasons with their initial team. (Tatum will bump that figure to 7% next season.) And for now, only five of the 100 top-five picks in that period are still with the team that

item.title: The belief overall is that Quentin Grimes will land …
item.description: The belief overall is that Quentin Grimes will land something at or below the midlevel exception, and ultimately, it will come from the 76ers. "I do think he stays there out of pressure," a West executive said. "You have to keep at least one of [Grimes or Oubre], if not both, after that trade. "And if they don't go into the tax, they're going to get crushed."

item.title: “Jeremy Lin was a great teammate,” Anunoby said. “I …
item.description: Anunoby, despite being robbed of an opportunity that some players 15 years his senior never got to experience, remembers that time fondly. He looks back and reflects on all that he learned from his teammates about how to be a pro, how to prepare at the sport’s highest level. The players on that team were so influential on who Anunoby is today that he wanted to list the entire roster, as many as he could recall while put on the spot, to show appreciation for what that experience meant to him as h

item.title: Tyrese Maxey is officially a girl dad. On Wednesday, …
item.description: Tyrese Maxey is officially a girl dad. On Wednesday, Myra Gordon announced on Instagram that her and the Philadelphia 76ers guard's daughter, Marvel, had been born May 14. Gordon had announced back in November that the two were expecting a child. Gordon also posted a photo of Maxey and herself with Marvel on her Instagram story. Marvel is Maxey and Gordon's first child together. Gordon played college basketball at the University of Alabama and Pepperdine.

item.title: The belief overall is that Grimes will land something …
item.description: The belief overall is that Grimes will land something at or below the midlevel exception, and ultimately, it will come from the 76ers. "I do think he stays there out of pressure," a West executive said. "You have to keep at least one of [Grimes or Oubre], if not both, after that trade. "And if they don't go into the tax, they're going to get crushed."

item.title: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's -28 plus-minus is tied with …
item.description: ESPN Insights: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's -28 plus-minus is tied with 2023 Joel Embiid for the lowest plus-minus by the NBA's MVP in a clinching opportunity in the play-by-play era (since 1997) ??

item.title: Saturday will mark the second Game 7 in NBA history …
item.description: ‪NBA Stat‬: Saturday will mark the second Game 7 in NBA history between two teams who each had a .750+ win pct in the regular season. The other came in the 1981 Eastern Conference Finals, when the Celtics beat the 76ers 91-90 to complete their comeback from a 3-1 series deficit. (via OptaSports) #NBA

item.title: Tyrese Maxey has plenty to celebrate this year and can …
item.description: Philadelphia 76ers guard Tyrese Maxey has plenty to celebrate this year and can now add one of the biggest milestones of all: becoming a girl dad. The six-year veteran announced the arrival of his daughter, Marvel, on social media Thursday evening. The pictures show the Sixers star in a hospital bed holding the newborn, while another shows Marvel in a crib with her name glowing on the wall above.

item.title: Cleveland's Mike Gansey and Sixers assistant general …
item.description: Cleveland's Mike Gansey and Sixers assistant general manager Jameer Nelson are regarded as the current leading candidates for the head of basketball operations post, which became available when Philadelphia fired Daryl Morey on May 12. As The Stein Line reported Monday, there is a growing expectation in league circles that Nelson will be elevated to general manager in Philadelphia at a minimum in the event he does not get the top job.

item.title: Sixers hire Mike Gansey as President of Basketball Operations
item.description: Shams Charania: Just in: Cleveland Cavaliers general manager Mike Gansey has agreed to become the new President of Basketball Operations for the Philadelphia 76ers, sources tell ESPN. Gansey -- who rose the ranks within the Cavaliers front office since joining in 2011-12 -- now lands the top executive position in Philadelphia.

item.title: He began working for the Cavs in 2012 and rose through …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Breaking: The Philadelphia 76ers are hiring Mike Gansey as president, sources told @hoopshype . Gansey was most recently general manager of the Cleveland Cavaliers. He began working for the Cavs in 2012 and rose through the ranks, including winning G League Executive of the Year.

item.title: Gansey and Nick U'Ren of the WNBA's Phoenix Mercury …
item.description: Gansey and Nick U'Ren of the WNBA's Phoenix Mercury were the finalists for the 76ers' president job and both had separate final-stage meetings with team ownership this week on the east coast before the decision to hire Gansey was made on Friday, sources said.

item.title: He was very involved in targeting guys like Sam …
item.description: Kevin O'Connor: Always heard good things about Mike Gansey. He was very involved in targeting guys like Sam Merrill and Max Strus — pro personnel types of decisions. Should be a good hire for the Sixers.

item.title: Tonight marks only the second time in NBA playoff …
item.description: Dan Weiss: Tonight marks only the second time in NBA playoff history that two teams who each won 62+ games in the regular season meet in a deciding Game 7, & the first time since the 1981 ECF when the Celtics defeated the Sixers en route to their 14th NBA Championship.

item.title: Special guests Franz Wagner (Orlando Magic) and …
item.description: Special guests Franz Wagner (Orlando Magic) and Eurocamp alumni Bogdan Bogdanović (Los Angeles Clippers) and VJ Edgecombe (Philadelphia 76ers) will share their experiences and personal paths to the NBA with the next generation of global talent.

item.title: The 19th edition of the adidas Eurocamp will take …
item.description: The 19th edition of the adidas Eurocamp will take place from June 5–7 in Treviso, serving as a premier pre-draft showcase for international basketball players aged 16 to 22. This year’s event features a high-profile lineup of special guests, highlighted by Orlando Magic forward Franz Wagner and Eurocamp alumnus Bogdan Bogdanovic. The German and Serbian players will be joined by fellow NBA star VJ Edgecombe to mentor the prospects gathered in Italy. Besides the active NBA players, the rosters for

item.title: Paul George going to the hall of fame???… me and the …
item.description: Ty Lawson: Quick question is Paul George going to the hall of fame???… me and the homies having a heated argument .. we going to let yall decide for us..

item.title: "I thought it was over, I ain't going to lie to you," …
item.description: Julian Champagnie's improbable journey from being unceremoniously cut by Philadelphia on Valentine's Day 2023 to reaching the NBA Finals as a key member of the Spurs came to mind as he held onto the ball at the end of Game 7 of the Western Conference Finals. "I thought it was over, I ain't going to lie to you," the forward said, recalling how emotional he was when the Sixers let him go as a 21-year-old undrafted rookie out of St. John's.

item.title: It's believed that Elton Brand, who worked as GM under …
item.description: Sixers assistant general manager Jameer Nelson is widely expected to be promoted to GM under Gansey, who will replace Daryl Morey and report for Harris Blitzer Sports & Entertainment president Bob Myers. It's believed that Elton Brand, who worked as GM under Morey, will be offered another position within the organization.

item.title: Andre Drummond: 'I am very happy for Jared McCain. I wish we kept him'
item.description: Andre Drummond: "I am very happy for Jared McCain. I wish we kept him, obviously, but I think he went to a good situation, a team that is going to help him develop and help him grow. I love the kid to death. Very, very good kid."

item.title: I cried. Nobody knows this, but I cried. I sat in my …
item.description: Andre Drummond: The first time was in Detroit, when I got traded to Cleveland. I think that was the first time I felt real sadness. I was genuinely sad because I didn’t want to leave. I felt like I had so much more to give to Detroit. Speaker 1: Yeah. I felt like I wanted to do so much more in that state. I wanted to end my career there. I’ve always said it: I wanted to play in Detroit my whole career, and I wanted to win a championship in Detroit. It was hard. It was hard. I cried. Nobody knows

item.title: “I remember one game, I over helped with somebody in …
item.description: Legion Hoops: Andre Drummond on LeBron: “I remember one game, I over helped with somebody in the corner and he was like don't f***ing go out there that mans shooting 32% from 3 go up to the top... I said how the f*** do you know what people shoot percentage wise?”

item.title: There is a growing anticipation that Prosper Karangwa …
item.description: There is a growing anticipation that Prosper Karangwa will elect to stay with the 76ers after the hiring of Mike Gansey as the team's new president of basketball operations. Karangwa is the Sixers' vice president of player personnel and has drawn interest in recent weeks from the Los Angeles Lakers and Dallas.

item.title: Andre Drummond: "My GOAT is Michael Jordan,
item.description: "Who is your GOAT?" Andre Drummond: "My GOAT is Michael Jordan, by default. My mom is a super Michael Jordan fan, so that is who I grew up hearing about all day and all night."

item.title: While athletes in tennis and even the NFL have double …
item.description: While athletes in tennis and even the NFL have double dipped in broadcasting and playing, the trend of active player contributors continues to gain steam in professional basketball. NBA players Kyle Lowry and Tyrese Haliburton joined Prime Video as athlete contributors. On the WNBA side, Kelsey Plum signed on as a player contributor for Prime, Sophie Cunningham is working as a studio analyst for USA Network and Caitlin Clark made multiple appearances on NBC’s studio show for NBA games.

item.title: “I think it’s becoming more attractive because it …
item.description: “I think it’s becoming more attractive because it gives players the opportunity to show the other side of their brains and not just a basketball playing side,” Lowry said. “It’s a lot that goes into being a professional athlete, especially a basketball player. You have to continue to play basketball, working (on) your craft, but you also gotta watch the film, study it, etc. Also having to explain what you’re doing, how you are preparing yourself to the everyday fan is fun.”

item.title: The Celtics took measures, both publicly and …
item.description: For starters, the rumors surrounding Jaylen Brown’s alleged discontent have quieted down for the past few weeks. The 29-year-old sparked all sorts of speculation about his future in early May, when he declared this his “most fun season” yet on a livestream just one day after Boston was bounced by the Philadelphia 76ers in the first round. Beyond the strangeness of that comment coming after such a bad postseason finish, it was the fact that he spent the vast majority of this season without longti

item.title: And it made the NBA’s championship look like a cheesy, …
item.description: Desperate to create some buzz, someone at NBA HQ in Olympic Tower — mercifully, no one can now remember exactly who, though the late commissioner David Stern’s name came up — pushed the idea to call the 1982 series between the Lakers and the Philadelphia 76ers “Showdown ’82.” It had a logo and everything. And it made the NBA’s championship look like a cheesy, made-up event that would run on ESPN The Ocho. (“Coming up after the Great Eastern Skeet Shooting Championships … it’s Showdown ’82!”)

item.title: When asked if the company uses humans or AI to write …
item.description: “VJ Edgecombe always seems one step ahead on the defensive end. He pokes his hands into the passing lanes with perfect timing, triggering the fast break the other way.” “VJ took a star turn in his sole season at Baylor, earning 2024/25 Big 12 Rookie of the Year honors. He ranked fourth in the conference with 68 steals.” When asked if the company uses humans or AI to write the blurbs on its cards, referring specifically to these Edgecombe descriptions, Panini said in a statement to The Athletic:

item.title: Outside of the four-straight Cavaliers-Warriors NBA …
item.description: Sports Media Watch: Outside of the four-straight Cavaliers-Warriors NBA Finals, Knicks-Spurs was the most-watched Game 1 of the Finals in 25 years (Sixers-Lakers 2001):

item.title: Thunder not interested in Giannis Antetokounmpo
item.description: As much as the idea of Thunder splurging for Giannis to counter Wemby might make sense on paper, league sources with knowledge of the Thunder's thinking continue to dismiss them as a legitimate Giannis Antetokounmpo suitor. The Thunder obviously have the draft capital and an abundance of talented young players to get Milwaukee's attention, but it's wise in times like these to consider Sam Presti's history. Presti is hardly known for paying exorbitant trade costs to bolster his roster ... let alo

item.title: 76ers promote Jameer Nelson to Executive Vice President of Basketball Operations
item.description: Shams Charania: The Philadelphia 76ers are promoting assistant general manager Jameer Nelson to the team's Executive Vice President of Basketball Operations under new President Mike Gansey, making the Philly native and former NBA All-Star the No. 2 executive for the franchise, sources tell ESPN. 76ers assistant GM Prosper Karangwa has also agreed to a multiyear contract extension.

item.title: Elton Brand will not return as 76ers general manager
item.description: Shams Charania: Sources said Elton Brand will not return as 76ers GM and is currently working through a new role with the franchise and its parent company, Harris Blitzer Sports & Entertainment. Brand, well-respected throughout the 76ers’ organization, elected not to interview for the President role after the team parted with Daryl Morey and has been fully supporting the team’s ongoing Draft prep.

item.title: VJ Edgecombe on Victor Wembanyama: Seeing him in person, he's not real
item.description: Is Victor Wembanyama even more ridiculous in person? VJ Edgecombe: Yeah, he's insane. Obviously, there's never been anyone around that's obviously as skilled as him at that height, so, yeah. I've seen him in person. I just think when you see somebody like that like in the court, what is this? Yeah, man. He's not real. I don't think he's real. [Laughs]

item.title: So yeah, just stuff like that. You got me. I told my …
item.description: Any rookie or 'welcome to the NBA' moments for you? VJ Edgecombe: When I was a primary defender for Ja Morant, he had 40... Yeah, I say that, I saw like that move of James Harden. You'd be like, 'Man, I won't foul him. If he does that, I won't foul him.' I fell for that, like he put my hand right in the cookie jar. So yeah, just stuff like that. You got me. I told my teammates before the game, I'm like, 'I won't get caught with that.' Go right there. Beep. Foul.

item.title: When it comes to financials or off the court or just …
item.description: Which veterans gave you the most guidance? VJ Edgecombe: Kyle Lowry, Eric Gordon before he got traded, PG, those three. They were the vets on the team. I ask a ton of questions. I'm the person who asks a ton of questions, 'cause I just wanna get better as a player and as a person. When it comes to financials or off the court or just buying stuff, housing, on the court, how to keep my body right. Like, what do you do? Like, what's your routine? Like, how do you find your routines? Just stuff like

item.title: Sixers' Mike Gansey: 'It's not a championship caliber team right now, but we're gonna work on that'
item.description: Derek Bodner‬: Mike Gansey: "It's not a championship caliber team right now, but we're gonna work on that....We're gonna do everything we can to bring players in that can fit, and help."

item.title: Mike Gansey on his roster-building philosophy: "I want …
item.description: ‪Gina Mizell‬: Mike Gansey on his roster-building philosophy: "I want fountains, not drains" as far as character/makeup of guys around the core of Embiid-Maxey-George-Edgecombe.

item.title: Mike Gansey said he’s had a lot of good conversations …
item.description: ‪Paul Hudrick‬: Mike Gansey said he’s had a lot of good conversations with Joel Embiid. Said Embiid is part of the group going forward and they have to make sure they keep their guys on the court.

item.title: VJ Edgecombe: I've played many different roles this …
item.description: VJ Edgecombe: I've played many different roles this year. I've been the first option, the second option, the third option, the fourth option. I've played so many different roles, but it helps. It all played into my favor because of, like I said, who my teammates are.

item.title: LeBron James is VJ Edgecombe's GOAT
item.description: Who is your GOAT and why? VJ Edgecombe: You have the longevity of LeBron's career, and that's why I say he's my GOAT, just because of his longevity, you know, sustaining, staying there at a high level throughout the whole time. Yeah, that's why. That's why he's my GOAT.

item.title: Bob Myers says 76ers 'got to find an identity'
item.description: Kyle Neubeck: Bob Myers: “we’ve got to find an identity, we just don’t have one”

item.title: Jameer Nelson: "In the past I've had opportunities to …
item.description: Derek Bodner: Jameer Nelson: "In the past I've had opportunities to talk to other teams. I want to be part of the solution here."

item.title: Jameer Nelson: "To do it for the home team gives me …
item.description: Derek Bodner: Jameer Nelson: "To do it for the home team gives me goosebumps." "Growing up in Chester and understanding what this team means to the city, I can help everyone in the building understand that."

item.title: "The goal is to get Joel on the floor as much as we …
item.description: Austin Krell: "The goal is to get Joel on the floor as much as we can. That's for us obviously to help find out and to accomplish. We all know when he's on the floor, he's the best big, in my eyes. He's one of the guys who, when healthy, everybody wants." - Jameer Nelson on Joel Embiid

item.title: 76ers expected to address shooting, rebounding during offseason
item.description: Austin Krell: Bob Myers was asked if the Sixers will make 3p shooting and rebounding points of emphasis this summer. "For sure." Myers went on to mention that defensive rebounding, specifically, was an area of concern.

item.title: Notable that Bob Myers explicitly mentioned the …
item.description: Austin Krell: Notable that Bob Myers explicitly mentioned the non-taxpayer mid-level exception as a tool at Sixers' disposal this summer. That, of course, is the bigger of the 2 MLE options and usually goes hand-in-hand with the bi-annual exception. Suggests Sixers will spend relatively significant money.

item.title: The latest is located in New York Midtown, between …
item.description: Kaysuane has put together two murals during the Knicks’ spectacular run through the playoffs. The latest is located in New York Midtown, between 30th Street and 31st Street and 8th Avenue, depicting Jalen Brunson as the “King of the East,” with NBA guards Trae Young, Tyrese Maxey and James Harden bowing to the throne. Next to the image is a Larry O’Brien championship trophy with the Knicks logo drawn over it. A Game 3 loss in the NBA Finals hasn’t taken the spirit from Kaysuane or the New York C

item.title: The biggest names on the roster are NBA …
item.description: Turkiye has announced its preliminary roster for the upcoming FIBA World Cup European qualifiers, with Ergin Ataman preparing a deep and talent-filled squad for the next international window. The Turkish national team will feature a mix of EuroLeague players, young prospects, and NBA talent as they continue their strong run in Group C. The biggest names on the roster are NBA players Alperen Sengun and Adem Bona. Ataman's squad also includes several experienced EuroLeague and national team regula

item.title: Chris Cenac Jr. says he met with the Knicks, Nets and …
item.description: Adam Zagoria: Houston’s Chris Cenac Jr. says he met with the Knicks, Nets and 76ers

item.title: Santa Clara F Allen Graves has not worked out for the …
item.description: Adam Zagoria: Santa Clara F Allen Graves has not worked out for the Knicks but did work out for the Sixers ‘The facilities are amazing. The city is beautiful.’ Projected late 1st-round pick.

item.title: If I have this right, in the final accounting, the …
item.description: Dan Devine: So, if I have this right, in the final accounting, the Thunder turned Paul George into SGA, Jalen Williams, Thomas Sorber, Brooks Barnhizer, Aday Mara and a protected Kings 2027 first-round pick? Decent deal!

item.title: Labaron Philon heads to Sixers at No. 22
item.description: Tony Jones: The Sixers' pick appears to be Labaron Philon, out of Alabama

item.title: Philadelphia 76ers will select Labaron Philon Jr. with …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Philadelphia 76ers will select Labaron Philon Jr. with the No. 22 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft.

item.title: Says he knows and has played against VJ Edgecombe in …
item.description: Adam Aaronson‬: Labaron Philon Jr. has never been to Philadelphia. "I've heard they've got great cheesesteaks." Says he knows and has played against VJ Edgecombe in high school and has loved watching him "go out there and kill" in the NBA. Said Edgecombe was great on "all levels."

item.title: Labaron Philon says he talked to Jameer Nelson and …
item.description: Austin Krell: Labaron Philon says he talked to Jameer Nelson and connected with the Sixers at the draft combine. Seems excited about playing with Maxey and Edgecombe.

item.title: One of those players, team sources who have been …
item.description: Connelly said on Tuesday night that the Timberwolves were trying to get back into the first round when a few players they liked started to fall, but they were unable to complete a deal. One of those players, team sources who have been granted anonymity to discuss nonpublic information said, was Alabama guard Labaron Philon, who went to Philadelphia at No. 22.

item.title: "Obviously I really enjoyed working with Daryl. I …
item.description: Austin Krell: Nick Nurse on Daryl Morey's departure: "Obviously I really enjoyed working with Daryl. I really did day-to-day and all that kind of stuff was very enjoyable. But it's a decision that's been made and now we got to move on from it. I wish him the best. Always have a fondness for Daryl. But Mike's here and he's in charge now and my job is to get this team better every day and that's what we're going to do."

item.title: "I knew a few off the top of my head, but I hadn't …
item.description: He will become the 13th player in NBA history to reach a 20th season, joining LeBron James, Vince Carter, Chris Paul, Dirk Nowitzki, Kevin Garnett, Kevin Willis, Robert Parish, Kyle Lowry, Udonis Haslem, Jamal Crawford, Kobe Bryant and Kareem Abdul-Jabbar. "I knew a few off the top of my head, but I hadn't seen the list," Al Horford told ESPN. "It's hard to believe. It's something that, for me, I'm very grateful. It's just so hard to be in this league for that amount of years. There's a lot of c

item.title: Labaron Philon, on returning for his sophomore season: …
item.description: ‪Derek Bodner‬: Labaron Philon, on returning for his sophomore season: "It was very important for me to go back, work on the shooting, and being able to play both on and off ball...That's one of my biggest traits, being able to adapt and do both."

item.title: "I got a call from my agent telling me first that we …
item.description: His path could have looked very different. Before choosing the Knicks, Yabusele said he had a similar offer from the Denver Nuggets following his stint with the Philadelphia 76ers. "I got a call from my agent telling me first that we had an offer from Denver and that it could be cool. It was a two-year deal, so I started thinking about that option. Right after that, my agent calls me and says, 'I just got off the phone with New York, and New York is also interested in pretty much the same deal,'

item.title: AJ Dybantsa on his NBA player comparisons: "Some …
item.description: AJ Dybantsa on his NBA player comparisons: "Some people say younger Paul George. I have heard a bigger Shai, I have heard a bigger Jalen Brunson. But the most accurate one I have ever got was Tracy McGrady. I think he was just super uber-talented, being 6'9, jumping over people to shoot, being a windmill, and being super athletic. I think that is kind of me."

item.title: Damjan Rudez on Andrew Wiggins: He doesn't have that high-energy drive that separates the superstars from the great players
item.description: Damjan Rudez: 'I cannot accurately describe how immense Andrew Wiggins' potential truly was. Having been on the court with him, I believe he had more raw potential than KAT, Zach LaVine, or 99% of the players I ever played with—even including guys like Paul George. There was a dunk he had against Atlanta where I swear I was looking at Michael Jordan. He would just rise and keep rising over three defenders. However, he didn't always have the 'motor'—that high-energy drive that separates the super

item.title: Sparking a series of organizational discussions that …
item.description: The Celtics had lost in the first round to the Philadelphia 76ers, sparking a series of organizational discussions that led to the conclusion that the current roster simply "wasn't good enough," as one Celtics source put it.

item.title: Vanderbilt basketball just added another player to the …
item.description: Vanderbilt basketball just added another player to the list of alumni in the NBA. Vanderbilt basketball guard Duke Miles has signed an Exhibit 10 undrafted free agent deal with the Philadelphia 76ers, his father, Marcus told Vandy On SI.

item.title: Labaron Philon Jr. says Alperen Sengun will be the …
item.description: [NBA2KL] Sixers rookie Labaron Philon Jr. says Alperen Sengun will be the first star whose ankles he breaks: “I be on TikTok a lot… they be saying he play like he got a refrigerator on his back.”

item.title: Cavs, 76eres, Lakers, Spurs, Suns, Pistons, Blazers among teams interested in Dean Wade?
item.description: Cleveland Cavaliers forward Dean Wade is expected to have various suitors in free agency, including the Cavaliers, Philadelphia 76ers, Los Angeles Lakers, San Antonio Spurs, Phoenix Suns, Detroit Pistons, and Portland Trail Blazers, league sources told HoopsHype.

item.title: Landale, 30, is expected to have a strong market, …
item.description: Landale, 30, is expected to have a strong market, including interest from the Hawks, Chicago Bulls, Los Angeles Lakers, Los Angeles Clippers, Philadelphia 76ers, and Cleveland Cavaliers, league sources told HoopsHype. Expect the Australian center to command offers north of the bi-annual exception in free agency.

item.title: "Joel wanted (LeBron James) on the Sixers, okay, back …
item.description: Law Murray: "Joel wanted (LeBron James) on the Sixers, okay, back in 2018. Like, really, really bad. And when LeBron left Cleveland for the Lakers, Joel had one of my favorite of his troll tweets when he was like, I'm paraphrasing, but he was like, they're not going to like you in Los Angeles. That's Kobe's town. And that dude LeBron, it's been that's how it's felt. Like he's been polarizing in a Los Angeles uniform because you got Bron fans, you got Lakers fans, you got Kobe fans. It's a whole

item.title: Spurs, Magic and Sixers interested in John Collins
item.description: League sources say John Collins certainly has fans in Minnesota, but it appears unlikely that the Timberwolves will have sufficient spending power to pursue the scoring forward, who spent last season with the Clippers. Collins is also expected to draw interest from Orlando, San Antonio and Philadelphia once free agency begins. The 76ers and Western Conference champion Spurs continue to get linked in free agency projections to the top forwards in the marketplace.

item.title: Clippers exploring re-signing John Collins amid interest from Spurs, Magic, and 76ers
item.description: We're also hearing that the Clippers might elect to try to re-sign forward John Collins, who as The Stein Line previously reported is expected to draw interest from the likes of San Antonio, Orlando and Philadelphia once free agency formally begins Tuesday night at 6 PM ET.

item.title: The Sixers will exercise the $3.4 million team option …
item.description: Jake Fischer: The Sixers will exercise the $3.4 million team option for Dominick Barlow, league sources tell @TheSteinLine .

item.title: 76ers to decline team option on Trendon Watford, allowing him to hit unrestricted free agency
item.description: Tony Jones: The Philadelphia 76ers have declined the team option on forward Trendon Watford, league sources tell The Athletic. Watford now becomes an unrestricted free agent

item.title: Josh Okogie is among a group of swingmen they are …
item.description: Sources say Josh Okogie is among a group of swingmen they are looking at to support the soon-to-be-back-from-injury Tyrese Haliburton. Indiana can pay part of the non-taxpayer midlevel to compete against other teams' prospective offers at the veteran's minimum and is also said to have Gary Trent Jr. and Kelly Oubre Jr. on its list.

item.title: Lakers interested in Quentin Grimes
item.description: Marc Stein: Last night we reported the Lakers' rising interest in Toronto free agent Sandro Mamukelashvili. Now league sources tell @JakeLFischer and me that Philadelphia's Quentin Grimes is emerging as another top Lakers target. MORE Intel here: tinyurl.com/bde3f33k

item.title: Lakers could potentially sign both Sandro …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: The Lakers could potentially sign both Sandro Mamukelashvili & Quentin Grimes while preserving a $41.25 million max slot. One could go into their surplus cap space (~$11 million, could create more via trade). The other could go into the $9.4 million room mid-level.

item.title: Quentin Grimes to get $10 million annually?
item.description: ‪Adam Aaronson‬: Jake Fischer says on his B/R stream that he expects Quentin Grimes to sign a contract in the range of $10 million annually. www.youtube.com/watch?v=gqJC...

item.title: Final numbers on Joel Embiid's three-year supermax …
item.description: ‪Adam Aaronson‬: Final numbers on Joel Embiid's three-year supermax extension: 2026-27: $57,736,350 2027-28: $62,355,258 2028-29: $66,974,166 (player option) Total: three years, $187,065,774

item.title: Kelly Oubre expected to meet with Sixers, Pacers, Blazers, Lakers, others
item.description: Kelly Iko: Free agent wing Kelly Oubre Jr. plans to meet with the 76ers, Pacers, Blazers and Lakers among others, sources tell @YahooSports.

item.title: Fischer: Lakers expected to get Sandro Mamukelashvili, Quentin Grimes
item.description: NBACentral: The Lakers are expected to land Sandro Mamukelashvili and Quentin Grimes, per @JakeLFischer "The whole league is operating as if Mamu and Quentin Grimes are done there" (h/t @hmfaigen )

item.title: Robert Williams' extension is only fully guaranteed in first year
item.description: Jake Fischer: Dean Wade's deal with the Sixers has a partial guarantee in Year 4, per source. In Portland, I'm told Robert Williams' extension is fully guaranteed at $14 million for this season, but just $5 million guaranteed in Year 2, which becomes fully guaranteed if he plays 50 games this 2026-27 campaign. Then, Year 3 is entirely non-guaranteed, but becomes fully guaranteed if Williams plays 50 games in 27-28. Wonky...

item.title: Other teams who showed significant interest in Wade …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Dean Wade's 4-year, $39 million deal with the 76ers includes a fourth-year partial guarantee. Other teams who showed significant interest in Wade included the Cleveland Cavaliers, Los Angeles Lakers, Los Angeles Clippers, and Detroit Pistons, sources said.

item.title: Dean Wade's contract will come out of the Non-Taxpayer …
item.description: Keith Smith: Dean Wade's contract will come out of the Non-Taxpayer MLE for the Philadelphia 76ers. They'll now be hard-capped at the first apron. Sixers have roughly $16.6M in space under the first apron with two open standard roster spots.

item.title: Dean Wade signs with Philadelphia
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent forward Dean Wade has agreed to a four-year, $39 million deal with the Philadelphia 76ers, sources tell ESPN. Several contenders pursued the former Cavaliers forward, who has become an indispensable defending and shooting frontcourt piece.

item.title: Unrestricted free agent Quentin Grimes will not be …
item.description: Unrestricted free agent Quentin Grimes will not be back in a Philadelphia 76ers uniform, according to league sources who spoke on condition of anonymity to describe ongoing deliberations.

item.title: Ariel Hukporti agrees to a one-year, $3.4 million deal with the Sixers
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent center Ariel Hukporti has agreed to a one-year, $3.4 million deal with the Philadelphia 76ers, sources tell ESPN. Hukporti departs the Knicks after playing a reserve role on their title team, and gives the 76ers frontcourt depth and a high-motor, athletic big man.

item.title: This is more than Ariel Hukporti's minimum salary, so …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: This is more than Ariel Hukporti's minimum salary, so this will likely come from the Sixers' mid-level exception. They have $6 million remaining after signing Dean Wade last night.

item.title: On Ariel Hukporti, heard Sixers like what they see in …
item.description: Ian Begley: On Ariel Hukporti, heard Sixers like what they see in the 24-year-old’s potential/upside, particularly as a rebounder, rim protector and floor-runner. They see the former Knick’s skillset as complementary to the way their guards push the ball/plays with pace. While we wait for Mitchell Robinson decision, Knicks will now have to find more depth at center with Hukporti in Philadelphia

item.title: Andre Drummond: I'm not willing to play for less than my worth
item.description: Derek Bodner: Andre Drummond reflects on being a free agent: "I love playing in the Association. It's a dream come true for me. But I'm also not willing to play for less than my worth." "I did that once and then I got labeled as one of those guys, and I think it really killed my value in the NBA, because I'm still moving like I'm in my mid-20s. I still have a lot left in the tank."
