"I think by taking that paycut...I feel like I've been climbing out of a hole for the last 4-5 years. It's been har

item.title: If Knicks remain committed to staying under 2nd apron, …
item.description: Ian Begley: If Knicks remain committed to staying under 2nd apron, and Andre Drummond remains committed to this approach in free agency, hard to see NYK & Drummond as a match. NYK & Kevon Looney entered free agency with mutual interest; NYK has also consistently signaled that they'd like Jordan Clarkson back

item.title: Kelly Oubre signs two-year deal with Pacers
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent forward Kelly Oubre Jr. has agreed to a two-year, nearly $17 million deal to sign with the Indiana Pacers, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: Jaylen Brown traded to Sixers
item.description: Shams Charania: BLOCKBUSTER: The Boston Celtics have agreed to trade Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers for Paul George, two first-round picks and two second-round picks, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: Salary cap situation relatively unchanged. George also …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: Boston Celtics after trading Jaylen Brown for Paul George and two first-round picks. Salary cap situation relatively unchanged. George also has a trade bonus that increases his $54.1 million salary to $57.7 million max. They have a surplus of 8 first-round picks and 7 seconds.

item.title: The 2028 pick Boston is getting, or swapping, with the …
item.description: Keith Smith: The 2028 pick Boston is getting, or swapping, with the Sixers is kind of complicated. Boston could get Philadelphia's pick if it lands 1-8. If that pick is 9-30 it's gone from the Sixers in a complicated swap that involves multiple teams. If the Philadelphia pick is 9-30, then Boston could get the most favorable of their own pick and the LA Clippers pick that Philadelphia owns, with the less favorable pick staying with the Sixers. I think that's how it will work out. More to come wh

item.title: Pretty clean swap of contracts
item.description: Bobby Marks: Pretty clean swap of contracts To Boston Paul George 2026-27: $54.1M 2027-28: $56.6M (Player) * Extension eligible To Philadelphia Jaylen Brown 2026-27: $57M 2027-28: $61M 2028-29: $64.9M * Eligible to sign a 1 year extension on 7/26

item.title: Details on 76ers picks to the Celtics, per sources:
item.description: Shams Charania: Details on 76ers picks to the Celtics, per sources: -- 2028 first-round pick that could convert from a first to a swap that is more favorable to Boston -- 2031 unprotected Philadelphia first -- 2028 second round, most favorable of GSW/OKC/MIL -- 2030 second, most favorable of WAS/PORT/PHX

item.title: Multiple league sources confirm that the Celtics have …
item.description: Adam Himmelsbach: Multiple league sources confirm that the Celtics have traded Jaylen Brown to the 76ers for Paul George, two first-round picks and two seconds, ending the Boston tenure of the 5-time All-Star and 2024 Finals MVP.

item.title: Philadelphia 76ers now have $500 million committed to …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: The Philadelphia 76ers now have $500 million committed to Joel Embiid, Tyrese Maxey, and Jaylen Brown over the next three seasons.

item.title: He has a $1.8 million trade bonus that will be divided …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: Sixers salary cap situation after trading Paul George and picks for Jaylen Brown. He has a $1.8 million trade bonus that will be divided over the next three years. He will be extension-eligible during the offseason for up to two additional years.

item.title: Before Jaylen, the last player to finish Top 6 in MVP …
item.description: HoopsHype: Jaylen Brown finished No. 6 in MVP voting in 2025-26. Before Jaylen, the last player to finish Top 6 in MVP voting and get traded the following offseason was... Paul George.

item.title: Not a single Jaylen-to-Sixers trade rumor on …
item.description: HoopsHype: FWIW: Not a single Jaylen-to-Sixers trade rumor on HoopsHype's Rumors page EVER.

item.title: Paul George's trade kicker will lift his salary to the …
item.description: One cap note from Boston's side on today's blockbuster: Paul George's trade kicker will lift his salary to the 35-percent max of $57,736,350 for the coming year, unless he waives it.

item.title: The nba is doing that THING AGAIN!
item.description: Tyrese Maxey: The nba is doing that THING AGAIN!

item.title: Sixers express interest in LeBron James
item.description: The Philadelphia 76ers have expressed interest in acquiring free agent forward LeBron James, a league source who spoke on condition of anonymity to describe ongoing deliberations told The Athletic.

item.title: Jaylen Brown never asked for a trade,
item.description: Chris Mannix: Jaylen Brown never asked for a trade, sources confirm to @SInow. Brown’s decade-long run—which included six conference finals, two NBA Finals and one championship—comes to a shocking end.

item.title: "This was not a trade that you would make in a …
item.description: NBA Base: There is speculation around the league that the Celtics’ trade sending Jaylen Brown to the 76ers was made ‘under duress,’ per @WindhorstESPN 😳 "This was not a trade that you would make in a clinical setting... You wouldn't do this trade under these circumstances if everything was equal, that something wasn't equal."

item.title: So, Boston either comes away with a decent pick …
item.description: Keith Smith: For full clarity, here are the various options for the 2028 swap/pick the Celtics got in the trade with the Sixers: 1. If LAC is 1-16 AND PHI is 1-8, then BOS gets the best of LAC/PHI/[lesser of BOS (top-1 protected)/SAS] 2. If LAC is 1-16 and PHI is 9-30, then BOS gets the best of LAC/[lesser of BOS (top-1 protected)/SAS] 3. If LAC is 17-30 and PHI is 1-8, then BOS gets the best of PHI/lesser of BOS (top-1 protected)/SAS] 4. If LAC is 17-30 and PHI is 9-30, then BOS keeps lesser of

item.title: There are connections with James and president of …
item.description: There are connections with James and president of basketball operations Mike Gansey. The two crossed over for a few years in Cleveland. LeBron was leading the franchise to its first ever NBA title while Gansey served as the team's director of development league operations before being promoted to assistant general manager. But their connection goes back a bit further. As we all know, LeBron was born in Akron, Ohio. Gansey is from Olmsted Falls, Ohio. As Mike's younger brother Steve reminded ever

item.title: Celtics agonized for days over the decision to trade Jaylen Brown
item.description: News of the deal sent shockwaves through the NBA and rattled a puzzled Celtics fan base Wednesday. One league source said Boston’s brass agonized for days over the decision to trade Brown but eventually determined that acquiring valuable assets along with a nine-time All-Star in George offered the franchise its best path forward.

item.title: Brown, who led the Celtics to 56 wins with Jayson …
item.description: One league source said Brown, who led the Celtics to 56 wins with Jayson Tatum sidelined for most of the season following an Achilles injury, never requested a trade and had grown frustrated with how the Celtics were handling this situation. The source said Wednesday that it was too early to know how Brown was processing this trade. Brown did not respond to a text message seeking comment.

item.title: But a league source stressed that this was not a …
item.description: George, 36, has two years remaining on his four-year, $211 million contract. He will make $54.1 million next season and has a $56.6 million player option for 2027-28. He has one less year on his contract than Brown, and the move will save Boston about $2.9 million this season. But a league source stressed that this was not a cost-cutting transaction, and there was no directive from ownership to trade Brown.

item.title: "What people are saying to me, Bobby, and this is just …
item.description: Brian Windhorst: "What people are saying to me, Bobby, and this is just the league speculation, is that this was a trade that was made under duress. This was not a trade that you would make in a clinical setting. There is no deadline, it didn't have to do it by the end of July 4th, that you wouldn't do this trade under these circumstances if everything was equal, if something wasn't equal."

item.title: Celtics originally wanted VJ Edgecombe from the 76ers
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Shams on the Jaylen Brown trade: "Sixers president reached out to the Celtics made offers in recent weeks...the Celtics had such a high bar on a deal. They were thinking if you're gonna trade us Paul George we need 4 firsts, 5 firsts, we need a player like a VJ Edgecombe. That was the bar that was set early. There were other teams around the league that might of stopped conversations or moved on to do other deals but the Sixers stayed with it...they stuck around and at the end o

item.title: Shapiro immediately reacted on X with a message, …
item.description: The political taunt came in immediate response to a breaking report from ESPN’s Shams Charania: “BLOCKBUSTER: The Boston Celtics have agreed to trade Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers for Paul George, two first-round picks and two second-round picks, sources tell ESPN,” Charania tweeted. Shapiro immediately reacted on X with a message, “Welcome to Philly, JB! Sixers get way better and, as a bonus, the Celtics got worse!”

item.title: The analytics-driven front offices believe that Jaylen …
item.description: [MacMahon] The simple fact is, the analytics-driven front offices believe that Jaylen Brown is significantly overpaid, and they don't value him as much as the media does. The media is the one who votes on all those awards.

item.title: Regardless of where you fall on the Jaylen Brown …
item.description: ‪Lev Akabas‬: Regardless of where you fall on the Jaylen Brown debate, it is pretty remarkable that the Boston Celtics were better with him off the court than on the court over his entire 10-year tenure with the team

item.title: Critique basketball all you want… but disease is …
item.description: Donovan Mitchell: Critique basketball all you want… but disease is insane… we gotta stop letting people just say whatever… cmon man! NBA Central: NBA sources believe Jaylen Brown thinks he’s the smartest person in the room, per @colincowherd: “I had two NBA sources … two people in the league, one an executive, one a scout, say that Jaylen Brown has — it’s a disease. He suddenly thinks he’s the smartest guy in every room he’s in … You make a lot of money, suddenly you’re absolutely sure, you don’

item.title: Celtics 'completely shut down' trade calls made over the past weeks on Jayson Tatum
item.description: The Boston Celtics never seriously considered trading Jayson Tatum despite recent inquiries from rival teams, according to ESPN's Shams Charania. Boston instead determined weeks ago that it needed to move on from Jaylen Brown, ultimately sending him to the Philadelphia 76ers for Paul George and two future first round picks. "Whether it was personal situations between the two players or whether it was just the on-court basketball fit, the Celtics had just made the determination that, moving forwa

item.title: Anfernee Simons joins 76ers
item.description: Shams Charania: Just in: Free agent guard Anfernee Simons has agreed to a two-year, $12.3 million deal with the Philadelphia 76ers, with a player option in second season, sources tell ESPN. Simons chose the 76ers over other suitors believing his fit is perfect with the revamped 76ers roster.

item.title: Jaylen Brown trade shocks NBA executives
item.description: Boston’s Jaylen Brown trade return of Paul George, two first-round picks, and two second-round picks left numerous NBA executives around the league bewildered. “They couldn’t have gotten better than that?” one neutral observing executive whose team was not in the Brown sweepstakes told HoopsHype. Many around the league were surprised George, 36, was the best player Boston could land for a 29-year-old Brown who was sixth in the NBA MVP voting this past season. George, a nine-time All-Star in his

item.title: Jaylen Brown says goodbye to Boston
item.description: Shams Charania: Jaylen Brown's statement after being traded from the Boston Celtics to the Philadelphia 76ers: First and foremost, thank you to the Most High, even in the midst of adversity. I'm here with gratitude. I’m still processing how this all went down. I’m excited and disappointed at the same time. I earned my respect from this city. I never asked for shortcuts or special treatment. I simply showed up every day, put my head down, and accepted every challenge. The relationships I built he

item.title: Jaylen Brown: As one chapter closes, another begins. …
item.description: Shams Charania: Jaylen Brown: As one chapter closes, another begins. I’m excited for what’s ahead and grateful for the opportunity to join Philadelphia. Every city has its own identity, its own passion, and its own expectation. I respect that, and I’m looking forward to earning that respect the only way I know how.. through the work. Philly - throw the ball up let’s get it!

item.title: Warriors, Heat and Nuggets among teams interested in Anfernee Simons before signing with Philadelphia
item.description: Michael Scotto: Anfernee Simons had interest from the Philadelphia 76ers, Golden State Warriors, Miami Heat, Denver Nuggets, Dallas Mavericks, and Indiana Pacers, league sources said. Simons ultimately agreed to a two-year, $12.3 million deal, including a player option, as ESPN first reported.

item.title: Philadelphia is a now a projected luxury tax team
item.description: Bobby Marks: Philadelphia is a now a projected luxury tax team. Roughly $3m below the first apron hard cap 14 players under contract Flexibility with 2 non/partials (Walker/Terry)

item.title: Tobias Harris says "urgency to win a championship" is why he joined Spurs
item.description: As for what factored ultimately in his decision to choose the Spurs, Harris told HoopsHype via text message, “The urgency to win a championship. It felt like both our goals aligned.”

item.title: Kyle Lowry will retire as a Toronto Raptor
item.description: Kyle Lowry will be retiring as a Toronto Raptor. One of the most significant players in franchise history and the backbone of the most successful era the Raptors have ever had will follow through on a long-time pledge to retire with the team he led for nine seasons from 2012-13 to 2020-21

item.title: Maxey, who shares the same Klutch Sports agency as …
item.description: NBA Base: The Philadelphia 76ers are expected to emerge as a strong suitor for LeBron James due to the Tyrese Maxey connection, per @WindhorstESPN Maxey, who shares the same Klutch Sports agency as LeBron, is viewed as a major recruiting tool for Philly.

item.title: Just enough to top any offers for the $6.1 million …
item.description: Strong value for Philadelphia to get Anfernee Simons for the remainder of their non-taxpayer mid-level exception, about $6.33 million this year — just enough to top any offers for the $6.1 million taxpayer MLE.

item.title: Boston has kicked the tires on Pelicans forward Trey …
item.description: The Celtics can spin that the pair of first-rounders they picked up have value. And they do. The 2028 one will likely end up as the Clippers’, who are beginning a rebuild with an Aspiration-sized anvil over their head. The ’31 pick is Philadelphia’s and, hey, who knows where the Sixers will be in four years. Boston has kicked the tires on Pelicans forward Trey Murphy III, sources say. This deal gives them more assets to pursue him.

item.title: I understand it may have been about financial reasons …
item.description: You saw this [Jaylen Brown] news and you thought what?" Robert Parish: "Ouch. First of all, I hated to see Jaylen being moved. I understand it may have been about financial reasons for moving him. I’m sure moving that contract gave the Celtics some more maneuvering room to add other pieces or new pieces. Understand that. I still do not like it. When I first heard about it, the first thing I thought about was the Dallas Mavericks when they moved Luka [Doncic], just historically a bad move. And I

item.title: Jaylen Brown on the process of Celtics trading him: 'There was a bit of a lack of respect'
item.description: Noa Dalzell: Jaylen Brown on the Celtics trading him, on his stream: "I wasn't thrilled with the amount of respect that was shown during this process. There was a bit of a lack of respect. I think it was fine at one point, and then... things just kind of went left."

item.title: "Boston, they packed me up."
item.description: Noa Dalzell: Jaylen Brown begins his statement by repeating this line a couple of times: "Boston, they packed me up."

item.title: "Who know what they’re about to get into off the …
item.description: Daniel Donabedian: Speaking of Jaylen Brown's enduring sense of humor, he said this about LaMelo Ball joining Anthony Edwards in Minnesota: "Who know what they’re about to get into off the court…They gotta get some supervision…Melo driving 100 miles per hour to the arena…and you already know what Ant on.” x.com/Big0Deuce/stat

item.title: "The Process is back the fuck on."
item.description: Erin Grugan: Jaylen Brown: "The Process is back the fuck on." "The Process been over, but now the Process is back motherfucker."

item.title: "I fuck with PG"
item.description: Law Murray: "I fuck with PG" -- Jaylen Brown, saying Paul George hooping vs Celtics in playoffs helped this deal go down

item.title: Jaylen Brown said he'll still host the Bridge program …
item.description: Noa Dalzell: Jaylen Brown said he'll still host the Bridge program in Boston this week and will continue to invest in Boston XChange Will also bring some of that work to Philadelphia

item.title: “I think I’m dead. The anesthesia killed me. Are there …
item.description: Bill Simmons on hearing about the Jaylen Brown trade from his wife picking him up from a colonoscopy: “I think I’m dead. The anesthesia killed me. Are there more first round picks?...I had a camera in my ass, and I had Paul George rammed up my ass”

item.title: "A key factor in any sort of Philadelphia pursuit of …
item.description: As one of Klutch Sports' premier clients, Maxey shares a strong connection with James that could help convince the 41-year-old to join Philadelphia. "A key factor in any sort of Philadelphia pursuit of LeBron is Tyrese Maxey — not only because of his talent, but because he is one of Klutch Sports' core clients and is basically part of LeBron's extended family," Windhorst said. "There's a longstanding relationship between Tyrese Maxey and LeBron because of Klutch. They recruited Maxey out of coll

item.title: "Maybe they end up rerouting Paul George elsewhere. …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Jovan: "Maybe they end up rerouting Paul George elsewhere. There's been talks about maybe them doing something with New Orleans. They have multiple firsts to put on the table to maybe go after Trey Murphy. Barring that this is a pretty surprising return for a guy of Jaylen Brown's caliber"

item.title: All the indications now are that the Celtics were not …
item.description: Shams Charania: "My understanding was Jaylen Brown felt disrespected, and the only way to recoup that relationship at that point was to give Jaylen Brown that two-year $145 million extension in July. But all the indications now are that the Celtics were not going to offer that. So then it became about seeing what can you get on the trade market."

item.title: "One executive told me this was always a little bit of …
item.description: FOX Sports' Colin Cowherd said league sources relayed intel to him regarding Brown, suggesting the 29-year-old has a "smartest guy in every room he's in" complex comparable to a "disease." "One executive told me this was always a little bit of Jaylen Brown's personality," Cowherd reported. "Kind of the smartest guy in the room, and livestreaming and throwing it out there — that's just not a good space. It used to be nothing good happens at three in the morning. Nothing good happens when pro athl

item.title: "I've already talked to some of the guys," Brown …
item.description: Several members of last season's Celtics reached out to Brown after hearing about the trade. Brown was contacted by various youngsters, labeled "unproven" by Stevens during last year's media day and ahead of the regular season. Brown expressed his gratitude for their words. "I've already talked to some of the guys," Brown revealed. "Some of the guys have already reached out from the Celtics and stuff like that. Some of the young guys, so I appreciate them. Because it was an honor this year. It s

item.title: Folks using “unnamed sources” to get off racist …
item.description: Jalen Rose: Folks using “unnamed sources” to get off racist @FCHWPO takes is wild. Take notes.

item.title: Then it was the assessment that they would be moving …
item.description: Shams Charania: "The Celtics made a couple of assessments. The first assessment was it was time to split up the duo of Jayson Tatum and Jaylen Brown. You cannot have both of those guys on supermax contracts. And then it was the assessment that they would be moving on clearly no matter what with Jaylen Brown. They were aggressive in seeking out packages, but when teams called on Jayson Tatum, they were given a complete no. When teams called on Jaylen Brown, they were wide open."

item.title: I know he's excited about, you know, he's been in …
item.description: Jaylen Brown: "Kessler to the Lakers. Walker, you know, Georgia kid, played with Game Elite. I know he's excited about, you know, he's been in Utah. I don't know how he feels about the big lights, the bright lights in LA, because I know Walker is more of a, he like a chill, cool."

item.title: "Personally, I think intelligence is relative," Brown …
item.description: In response, Brown cleared the air on that very perception. "Personally, I think intelligence is relative," Brown clarified. "There's different forms of what intelligence looks like — creative, divergence, there's being able to adapt. There's so many different forms of intelligence. Whatever you value is whatever you value. I never told nobody, 'Look, I'm the smartest person in the room.' That may be how they took it."

item.title: "In sports, no offense to everybody in sports, but the …
item.description: In the most humble way possible, Brown felt the need to "keep it buck" when it came to this topic Cowherd brought to light. "In sports, no offense to everybody in sports, but the bar is f*cking low. The bar isn't that high, so I think that's what people need to take into consideration."

item.title: "The guys that I was able to build with, those guys …
item.description: "The guys that I was able to build with, those guys that reached out to me, just told me — Neemy (Queta), Luka (Garza), all those guys told me 'It was a pleasure to play with you, and I appreciate everything that you helped with.' So that's the stuff that gives you more motivation to keep going. So I'm grateful, and it was a pleasure. If this was my last year as a Celtic, this was an awesome year."

item.title: Brown also received a surprise call from new Sixers …
item.description: Brown also received a surprise call from new Sixers teammate V.J. Edgecombe during the stream. “You on stream gang, it’s JB,” Brown said after picking up the phone. “I know it’s JB, bruh. I got your number,” Edgecombe replied while Brown laughed.

item.title: Jaylen Brown blasts anonymous sourcing in NBA media: 'They don't see how unethical that is'
item.description: Jaylen Brown: "Let's just stop with the anonymous sources. Chat, the anonymous sources is crazy. Him saying that executive and a scout, that said these things in an anonymous source, Stephen A. Smith saying an anonymous source, Kendrick Perkins saying an anonymous source, or somebody texting, like what is with these people and these anonymous sources, bro? They don't see how unethical that is and just stating that on live TV. It's like somebody saying a rumor in front of millions of people and n

item.title: "It's a lot of gaslighting and manipulation and …
item.description: Jaylen Brown: "It's a lot of gaslighting and manipulation and narrative control, and if you call it out, they attack you and call you and like arrogant and all these other type of things. But I get it, it's all good."

item.title: 76ers agree to sign Rayan Rupert to a two-way contract
item.description: Shams Charania: The Philadelphia 76ers have agreed to sign French guard Rayan Rupert on a two-way NBA deal, sources tell ESPN. Rupert, a second-round pick in 2023, split last season in Memphis and Portland.

item.title: Blazers' analytics rate Jaylen Brown as a 'negative player'
item.description: Another team with widely reported interest, the Trail Blazers, similarly did not join the chase. A Blazers team source said the Blazers were never enamored with Brown, even before Boston made it known he was available on the trade market. Two factors went into the Blazers’ disinterest: Their analytics viewed him as a negative player and the Celtics’ asking price was too high. “We were never aggressively looking to trade for him,” a team source said. “And particularly not at their price.”

item.title: Even more pointedly, a current general manager, …
item.description: The cool market suggested the existence of a significant gap between the general perception of Brown, who finished sixth in MVP voting this season, and the way he is seen within the NBA. Though the idea of him being viewed as the seventh-best player on a given team was widely mocked, a recent report that one front-office member saw Brown in that way illuminated the reality that advanced analytics never shined too brightly on Brown’s game. Even more pointedly, a current general manager, speaking

item.title: You saw this trade and let's be honest the initial …
item.description: Tim MacMahon: The league does not perceive him to be, the league in terms of the people who are making decisions on trade, that the league does not perceive him to be the sixth most valuable player in the league, because in part of his contract and then this is such a like hot button issue, but the advanced analytics are not favorable to Jaylen Brown. I had another GM basically say, "Look… Brian Windhorst: Here we go… Be careful what you're about to say. That's all I'm going to say. MacMahon: It

item.title: Robert Parish on Jaylen Brown trade to Sixers: 'If Red Auerbach was alive he may have thrown up'
item.description: SiriusXM NBA Radio: “If Red [Auerbach] was alive... he may have thrown up about this move”. Robert Parish tells @WorldWideWob what the legendary coach would think.

item.title: Lakers interested in Andre Drummond, Jonas Valanciunas, Kevon Looney
item.description: Shams Charania: The Lakers will now focus on finding Walker Kessler's backup on the market -- with centers such as Andre Drummond, Jonas Valanciunas and Kevon Looney among those being considered, sources said. The Wizards took a patient approach to the start of free agency and now land a durable, reliable center to join Anthony Davis and Alex Sarr in the frontcourt.

item.title: Caleb Love to 76ers
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent guard Caleb Love has agreed to a two-way NBA deal with the Philadelphia 76ers, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: Draymond Green on Jaylen Brown for Paul George trade: No disrespect to Paul George, but this isn’t an even swap
item.description: Draymond Green on Jaylen Brown for Paul George trade: No disrespect to Paul George, but this isn’t an even swap. I got a lot of love for PG. but like myself, PG is 36 years old. Not like myself. I’m going into year 15. I think PG is going into year 17. And Jaylen Brown, not even 30 years old. He’s 29 years old and he’s going into year 11. So, and by the way, he won finals MVP two years ago. I think he just made All-NBA second team this year. Was arguably controversially possibly could have been

item.title: A name that's come up when talking to rival teams …
item.description: Dan Woike: A name that's come up when talking to rival teams about a player who would fit the Lakers' current roster has been Andre Drummond. Interesting to see how the Lakers navigate the back-up center spot.

item.title: LeBron James' odds of playing for the Philadelphia …
item.description: LeBron James' odds of playing for the Philadelphia 76ers in 2026-27 have surged from 8.2% to 18.2% in the past 6 hours on Polymarket, a 10-point move.

item.title: Rich Paul has spoken to 27 teams about LeBron James
item.description: Paul told ESPN on Friday that he has now spoken to 27 teams about James and that the factors the veteran has to think about are fluid -- such as the Philadelphia 76ers trading for Jaylen Brown, for instance. "Every day things change," Paul told ESPN. "This is the first time that LeBron James is making a decision pressure-free. He's won already. He's made good on his promise -- he won in L.A. This is strictly for his happiness. What does happiness entail? It's a number of things. It's a bucket of

item.title: Paul added that an "X factor" for Philadelphia is its …
item.description: Paul added that an "X factor" for Philadelphia is its front office featuring executive vice president of basketball operations Jameer Nelson -- who was a contemporary of James, playing together on the All-Star team and against one another in the playoffs -- and president of basketball operations Mike Gansey -- who played against James in high school and was a member of the Cleveland Cavaliers' front office during James' second stint with the franchise. Paul also said he and James "love" the Sixe

item.title: Knicks targeted Andre Drummond due to his veteran …
item.description: Ian Begley: Knicks targeted Andre Drummond due to his veteran experience in the league, strong rebounding ability and his playoff experience, per source familiar with the matter. Club will lean on former Mount Vernon star to backup Karl-Anthony Towns. Knicks have roughly $5M left under second apron

item.title: Sam Cassell on Jaylen Brown trade: 'He has done everything possible for this city, he'll be missed, but it's the NBA'
item.description: Daniel Donabedian: Celtics assistant coach Sam Cassell on the Jaylen Brown trade: “Jaylen has done everything possible for this city. He’ll be missed. But it’s the NBA. Wilt Chamberlain got traded after he averaged 50 points one year…I wish nothing but the best for Jaylen. He’s a hell of a basketball player.” He then spoke on Paul George: “He doesn’t have to try to be Jaylen Brown. He’s gotta be Paul George…We’ll be fine.”

item.title: Shams: LeBron James deciding between Cavs, Heat, Nuggets, Sixers, Wolves, Warriors
item.description: Fullcourtpass: LeBron James will be making his free agent decision between the following six NBA teams, per @ShamsCharania - Cavaliers - Heat - Nuggets - Sixers - Timberwolves - Warriors

item.title: "To the Knicks fans, I know what this city expects, …
item.description: SNY Knicks: Andre Drummond releases a statement thanking the city of Philadelphia and addresses Knicks fans, as well: "To the Knicks fans, I know what this city expects, and I'm ready to embrace it. I'm coming in with gratitude, hunger, and a mindset to work. Let's make this chapter special." (via drummond/IG)

item.title: "9 years! Forever grateful for all that we …
item.description: Jayson Tatum: "9 years! Forever grateful for all that we accomplished together, for pushing me to be a better player. From first-round exits to winning a chip together. I'm thankful for it all. Nothing but love and respect for you as a player and as a person! Looking forward to see how you attack this next chapter of your career and wish nothing but the best for you! Continue to be special!"

item.title: Paul Pierce on the inevitability of the Jaylen Brown …
item.description: Paul Pierce on the inevitability of the Jaylen Brown trade: "It's like if you have a family member who is dying and it's like when they die you saw it coming. It sucks, but you know, when it happens it's like out of the blue, it's different. You know what I'm saying? So it was like, all right. So I came to acceptance of it that was going to happen although I don't like it."

item.title: Only 12 players have more wins in Boston than Brown. …
item.description: Jorge Sierra: Jaylen Brown won more games with Boston than Dave Cowens. (Cowens finished his Celtic career with two titles, one MVP and eight All-Star selections). Only 12 players have more wins in Boston than Brown. The youngest of those 12 are Paul Pierce and Larry Bird.
